title: More Nonsense Limerick 57
content:
There was an old man of Dumblane,
    Who greatly resembled a crane;
    But they said, "Is it wrong, 
    Since your legs are so long,
    To request you won't stay in Dumblane?"
meaning:
Edward Lear's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "More Nonsense Limerick 57"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
